mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing equity markets to price in lower future earnings and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer spending and construction investment, driving positive corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary easing, a monthly increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term positive equity repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during easing periods, or the predictive power reverses to negative during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly correlate with expanding economic activity and corporate hiring, which supports higher aggregate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 315.0, accelerating inflation forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 315.0 consistently coincide with positive 20-day equity returns, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal income confirms broad-based demand expansion, reducing recession risk and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal fails to predict positive quarterly returns, or the interaction effect is dominated by single-series noise in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, prompting equity markets to price in lower corporate profits and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines are followed by positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during early-cycle phases.
